mission.title: falco: Memory leak in modern-bpf driver on Bottlerocket OS causing frequent OOMKills
mission.description: Memory leak in modern-bpf driver on Bottlerocket OS causing frequent OOMKills. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify falco troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your falco deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco -n falco --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `libelf.h: No such file or directory`

steps.title: Review falco configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant falco configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco
kubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco
```
Falco 0.41.3 with `modern-bpf` driver experiences severe memory leaks on AWS Bottlerocket OS, consuming 40-50 MiB/minute and causing frequent OOMKills. This makes Falco unusable on Bottlerocket clusters in production environments.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Memory leak in modern-bpf driver on Bottlerocket OS causing…
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
Time      Pod A      Pod B      Pod C
T+0min    116Mi     122Mi      133Mi
T+2min    224Mi     243Mi      147Mi  
T+4min    269Mi     280Mi      158Mi
Growth:   ~38Mi/min ~53Mi/min  ~8Mi/min
```

steps.title: Confirm Memory leak in modern-bpf driver on Bottlerocket… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco -n falco --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `libelf.h: No such file or directory` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco/issues/3643
codeSnippets:
Time      Pod A      Pod B      Pod C
T+0min    116Mi     122Mi      133Mi
T+2min    224Mi     243Mi      147Mi  
T+4min    269Mi     280Mi      158Mi
Growth:   ~38Mi/min ~53Mi/min  ~8Mi/min

OOMKilling container "falco" in pod "falco-xxxx"
Exit code: 137 (OOMKilled)
Restart count: 21 (example pod)

Falco version: 0.41.3 (x86_64)
Falco initialized with configuration file: /etc/falco/falco.yaml
Loading rules from file /etc/falco/falco_rules.yaml

Starting internal webserver, listening on port 8765

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with falco installed or the issue environment reproducible.
